provenance.title: Attention Is All You Need
provenance.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.
key_claims: Transformer is the first sequence transduction model relying entirely on self-attention, with no recurrence or convolution.
Achieves 28.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 EN-DE, surpassing all prior models including ensembles by >2 BLEU.
Achieves 41.8 BLEU on WMT 2014 EN-FR as a single model at 1/4 the training cost of prior SOTA.
Transformer is significantly more parallelizable, allowing training in 12 hours on 8 P100 GPUs (base model).
Generalizes to English constituency parsing, achieving 92.7 F1 in semi-supervised setting.

modules.name: InputEmbedding
modules.notes: Weights multiplied by sqrt(d_model). Weight matrix shared with output embedding and pre-softmax linear.

modules.name: OutputEmbedding
modules.notes: Shares weight matrix with InputEmbedding and PreSoftmaxLinear.

modules.name: PositionalEncoding
modules.notes: PE(pos,2i)=sin(pos/10000^(2i/d_model)), PE(pos,2i+1)=cos(pos/10000^(2i/d_model)). Applied to both encoder and decoder inputs.

modules.name: EncoderLayer
modules.notes: Two sub-layers: MultiHeadSelfAttention + LayerNorm, then FFN + LayerNorm. Output = LayerNorm(x + Sublayer(x)).

modules.name: MultiHeadSelfAttention_Encoder
modules.notes: Q, K, V all come from same source (self-attention). No mask applied.

modules.name: FeedForward_Encoder
modules.notes: FFN(x) = max(0, xW1+b1)W2+b2. Applied identically and independently to each position.

modules.name: DecoderStack
modules.notes: Stack of N=6 identical DecoderLayers. Also receives encoder memory [B, T, 512].

modules.name: DecoderLayer
modules.notes: Three sub-layers: MaskedMultiHeadSelfAttention + LN, MultiHeadCrossAttention + LN, FFN + LN.

modules.name: MaskedMultiHeadSelfAttention_Decoder
modules.notes: Causal mask applied: positions cannot attend to subsequent positions. Implemented by setting illegal softmax logits to -inf.

modules.name: MultiHeadCrossAttention_Decoder
modules.notes: Q from previous decoder sub-layer; K and V from encoder output. Enables attending over all input positions.

modules.name: LayerNorm
modules.notes: Applied after each residual sub-layer in both encoder and decoder. Reference: Ba et al. 2016.

modules.name: PreSoftmaxLinear
modules.notes: Weight matrix shared with InputEmbedding and OutputEmbedding.

mathematical_spec.name: multi_head_attention
mathematical_spec.notes: W^Q_i in R^{d_model x d_k}, W^K_i in R^{d_model x d_k}, W^V_i in R^{d_model x d_v}, W^O in R^{h*d_v x d_model}. Base: h=8, d_k=d_v=64.

mathematical_spec.name: learning_rate_schedule
mathematical_spec.notes: Linearly increases LR for first warmup_steps, then decays proportionally to inverse sqrt of step number. warmup_steps=4000.

tensor_semantics.name: src_padding_mask
tensor_semantics.notes: Shape inferred from standard practice; paper does not explicitly describe padding mask shape.

training_pipeline.effective_batch_size: ~25000 source tokens + 25000 target tokens per batch
training_pipeline.curriculum: Sentence pairs batched by approximate sequence length.

special_conditions: Beam search: beam_size=4, length_penalty alpha=0.6 for translation.
Base model: average of last 5 checkpoints (10-min intervals).
Big model: average of last 20 checkpoints.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Weight initialization follows Xavier/Glorot uniform initialization for linear layers.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Literature default for Transformer implementations; paper does not specify.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Dropout is applied to attention weights (attention dropout) as well as sub-layer outputs (residual dropout), both at rate P_drop=0.1.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Section 5.4 describes 'dropout to output of each sub-layer' and 'sums of embeddings and positional encodings'. Attention dropout is standard practice and implied.
alternatives: Dropout only on residual path, not attention weights

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Framework: PyTorch (primary) or TensorFlow. Original code is tensor2tensor (TensorFlow).
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper cites github.com/tensorflow/tensor2tensor. Modern reproductions use PyTorch.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Padding mask applied to source sequence in encoder self-attention and decoder cross-attention to ignore padding tokens.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard NLP practice; paper does not explicitly describe masking strategy for padding.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Vocabulary is shared between encoder and decoder (described as 'shared source-target vocabulary' for EN-DE).
implementation_assumptions.basis: Section 5.1: 'shared source-target vocabulary of about 37000 tokens' for EN-DE.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Cross-entropy loss with label smoothing epsilon=0.1 is the training objective.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Section 5.4 explicitly states label smoothing value epsilon_ls=0.1.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Autoregressive decoding at inference (teacher forcing during training).
implementation_assumptions.basis: Section 3.1 states 'auto-regressive [10], consuming the previously generated symbols as additional input'. Standard encoder-decoder training.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Effective batch size of ~25k source + ~25k target tokens achieved via token-based batching, not fixed sample count.
implementation_assumptions.basis: Section 5.1: 'Each training batch contained a set of sentence pairs containing approximately 25000 source tokens and 25000 target tokens.'
alternatives: Fixed batch size of 64 or 128 samples

ambiguities.description: Paper says dropout applied to 'output of each sub-layer' and 'sums of the embeddings and positional encodings'. Unclear if attention weights themselves receive dropout.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Attention dropout at same rate as residual dropout (0.1)

ambiguities.description: Paper states weight matrix is shared between two embedding layers and pre-softmax linear. Unclear whether the encoder input embedding and decoder input embedding are the same matrix (i.e., three-way tie).
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Three-way weight tying: encoder_embed = decoder_embed = pre_softmax_linear^T
alternatives: Only decoder_embed and pre_softmax_linear are tied

ambiguities.description: Batch size per GPU vs effective batch size. '25000 source tokens' is the total batch, but token count per GPU and gradient accumulation strategy are unspecified.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: No gradient accumulation; full batch fits on 8 GPUs with data parallelism.

ambiguities.description: Sinusoidal PE added to embeddings before dropout or after? Order slightly ambiguous.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: PE added to embeddings, then dropout applied to the sum.
